executive_summary.verdict: Solver works but has significant numerical dissipation
key_findings: Full NS has numerical dissipation that scales with N
Inviscid case shows ~0.1% dissipation per 10 steps (acceptable)
Viscous case shows 0.13-5.8% dissipation per 10 steps (scales with N)
Root cause: Advection term introduces numerical diffusion from QTT truncation

root_cause_diagnosis.issue: Numerical dissipation from advection term scales with grid size
root_cause_diagnosis.mechanism: QTT truncation after Hadamard products loses high-frequency information
root_cause_diagnosis.velocity_reconstruction: poisson_iterations=0 skips Poisson solve, uses velocity diffusion approx
root_cause_diagnosis.impact: Excessive energy decay at large grids, but solver remains stable

recommendations: For production: Use 32³-64³ grids where numerical dissipation is <2%
For 128³+: Accept higher dissipation or increase rank to 32
Future work: Implement spectral Poisson solver for velocity reconstruction
Future work: Reduce Hadamard truncation aggressiveness in advection terms
diffusion_only=True mode works well for heat equation / pure diffusion
